claim_bullets.bullet_text: JavaScript uses the 'call by value' evaluation strategy, where expressions are evaluated before functions are applied (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.40-41).
In the example ((diameter) => diameter * 3.14159265)(1 + 1), the expression 1 + 1 is evaluated first to 2, then applied to the function (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.40-41).
While JavaScript always calls by value, the notion of a 'value' has additional subtlety, which will be discussed later (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.40-41).

source_record_text: JavaScript Allongé discusses the call by value evaluation strategy in JavaScript, where expressions are evaluated before functions are applied, and provides an example with the expression 1 + 1 being evaluated to 2 before being applied to a function.
